AccessLevel.description:
Access level for plugin-provided resources (stores, events, action prefix).
Controls who can consume the resource. Part of the Plugin Interaction Model.

oneOf.description: Any declared consumer can access. Bundled/same-author auto-approved, third-party needs user approval.
Only plugins from the same author can access. Auto-granted, no user prompt.
Only the providing plugin itself can access. No external access.

ActionFieldSchema.description: Schema for a single field within an action type.

display.description:
Optional display role for generic UI rendering. See `FieldDisplay`.
Currently consumed by the Collections tab's log-kind timeline view
to pick which fields appear in the row summary.

enum_values.description: Allowed string values for `field_type: "enum"`. Ignored otherwise.

field_type.description: Declared type for this field. See `FieldType`.

fields.description:
Nested field list for `field_type: "object"` (recursive). Ignored
for other field types.

ActionTypeSchema.description:
Schema declaration for a plugin-defined action type.
Enables generic UI rendering (structured editor fields) for any plugin's actions.

fields.description: Ordered list of fields for this action type.

modes.description:
Supported interaction modes: "tap" (single press), "hold" (start/stop via phase),
and/or "toggle" (start/stop cycle). Defaults to ["tap"] if omitted.

Anchor.description: Anchor position for a HUD window on screen.

Cardinality.description:
For the `tag` preset only — picks between singleton flag and multi-record.
Resolves `id_strategy` and `merge` at preset expansion. Other presets pin
these slots and ignore cardinality.

oneOf.description:
One record per collection. Writes replace; gate string is the
collection name (or the singleton record's name field if declared).

Many records keyed by a `name` field. Multiple records coexist;
gate strings are formed from the collection name + each record's name.

anyOf.description: File path relative to plugin directory (e.g. "commands.json").
Inline JSON data (e.g. keybind combos, configuration objects).

CollectionDataSource.description:
Data source for a collection contribution. A string is a file path relative to the plugin
directory; anything else (object, array) is inline JSON data.

CollectionSchema.description:
Unified schema for a collection.

Phase F: legacy `kind` enum + `key_field`/`value_field`/`id_field`/
`default_recording_enabled`/`retention_days` fields removed. Every
declaration uses the state shape: `preset` + slot overrides
(storage / writers / merge / lifecycle / feeds_matching / etc).

access.description: Read access level. Default `open`. (Phase F: subsumed by `privacy.access_level`.)

cardinality.description: State policy slot — meaningful only with `preset: "tag"`.

clear_on_unrelated_command.description:
Auto-clear all gates in this namespace when a command matches that
does not write a tag here (i.e. the user has "moved on" mid-session).
Sub-sprint 6.4: replaces voice's inline auto-clear in matchWords with
a manifest-declared trigger resolved server-side in `commands.match`.
Only meaningful for `feeds_matching: AsGates { scoped: true }` —
validation rejects the flag on other shapes. Distinct from
`Lifecycle::ClearOnEvent` because the trigger is conditional on the
matched command's tag effects, not a bus event.

clears_when_cleared.description:
Tags whose clearing should automatically clear this tag too. Used
for dependent state that's only meaningful while a parent gate is
held — most commonly mid-capture mode tags (`sets_on_partial`)
whose lifetime is bounded by their parent's `requires_tags`. For
the common case the platform auto-infers the relationship from
`requires_tags` + `sets_on_partial`; this field is an escape hatch
for cascades where the parent isn't in `requires_tags`. See
`notes/DESIGN_TAG_CASCADE_CLEAR.md`.

denylist.description:
`policy.denylist` — declares the collection's writes are subject
to the user's soft denylist toggle. The user toggle lives in the
`_platform.frozen_writers` collection, not the manifest.

description.description: Human-readable description of this collection's purpose.

emits_on_change.description: State policy slot — whether mutations emit collection.updated events.

exclusive.description:
When true, the gate becomes an exclusive gate: while active, only
commands whose `requires_tags` includes this gate can match.
All other commands are suppressed. Used for sub-command modes
(e.g. snap directions) where only the displayed options should work.

feeds_matching.description: State policy slot — matching-engine consumption opt-in.

fields.description:
Field schema for records. Used by all presets that have structured
payloads (named_entities, log, data, etc.).

id_strategy.description: State policy slot — id strategy override.

label.description:
Short human-readable name for this collection, shown in authoring UIs
in place of the raw collection id (e.g. a gate offered as a command
context reads "Browser active" instead of "plugin.browser.active").
Falls back to the collection id when unset.

lifecycle.description: State policy slot — record lifecycle (TTL, event-clear, retention).

merge.description:
How contributions from multiple plugins are merged when read.
In the new shape this is a policy slot; in the legacy shape it
was a top-level field. Same field, same wire key.

ordering.description: State policy slot — record ordering for list/subscribe.

preset.description:
Manifest-level shorthand. When set, expands to a default policy
bundle at parse time; per-slot fields below override the bundle.

privacy.description: State policy slot — privacy posture (recording, access level).

requires_tag.description:
`policy.requires_tag` — write only commits while the named tag
is in the active gate set.

ui_render.description: State policy slot — Settings UI render mode.

ConflictResolution.description:
Conflict-resolution mode declared on an `EffectDeclaration`. v1
ships only `ExclusiveLastWriter`; future variants (first-writer,
composable) join when concrete demand surfaces, per the design
doc's algebra-not-taxonomy principle.

oneOf.description:
Most-recent assertion holds top-of-stack ownership. When the top
retracts, the previous asserter auto-restores. Matches the
mode-shaped first-wave consumers (Focus, Meeting, Presentation,
Recording Mode).

Consumes.description: What a plugin consumes from the ecosystem.

collections.description: Collection names this plugin reads (declares read-dependency on the introducer).

events.description: Event patterns this plugin subscribes to.

writes.description:
Collection names this plugin writes to at runtime via collection.push
(cross-plugin runtime writes). Not needed for collections the plugin
introduces via `provides.collections` or contributes to via `collection_data`.

ContextNamePattern.description:
One `provides.context_names` entry. The pattern matches when both:
- every tag in `tags` is present in the live active-tag set
  (subset semantics — the live set may carry additional tags)
- `prefix_shape` matches the captured prefix positionally: literal
  strings match the corresponding token verbatim; `<collection_name>`
  placeholders match any token drawn from the named collection

Tiebreaking when multiple patterns match: longest `tags` wins;
ties break to the longer/more-specific `prefix_shape`; remaining
ties break lexicographically on `name` for determinism.

name.description: Friendly dotted name surfaced in logs (e.g. `"browser.hints"`).

prefix_shape.description:
Positional shape the captured prefix must match. Each element
is either a literal word or a `<collection_name>` placeholder.

tags.description: Tags required for this pattern to match.

DenylistPolicy.description:
`policy.denylist` — manifest affordance.

The manifest declares the affordance ("the user may freeze writes from
this plugin to this collection"); the user toggles the per-plugin gate
in Settings → Plugins → Inspector. v1 ships a single boolean affordance;
future versions could add per-collection scoping or
"always denylisted" claims.

user_toggleable.description:
Reserved. Always treated as `true` in v1 — the affordance is its
presence on the manifest. Future-compat slot if the policy grows
`mode` / `scope` knobs.

Dependency.description:
A plugin dependency with optional version constraint and source hint.
Accepts either a bare string ("keyboard") or an object ({"plugin": "keyboard", "version": ">=1.0.0", "source": "github:owner/repo"}).

source.description:
Explicit source for resolution (e.g. "github:owner/repo").
Optional — if omitted, resolved via catalog then installed plugins.

version.description:
Semver version constraint (e.g. ">=1.0.0", ">=1.0.0 <2.0.0").
Optional — omitted means any version.

DispatchVia.description: How a plugin determines dispatch targets.

oneOf.description: Dispatches whatever the matching engine returns.
Dispatches structured Plugin actions by type prefix.
Constructs action strings directly; must also declare `dispatch_prefixes`.

anyOf.description: Bare string — sugar form. Equivalent to `Object { name, args: None }`.

Structured form with optional `args` (reserved for future
parameter support; v1 rejects non-empty args at validation time).

EffectAssertEntry.description:
One entry in an `asserts` array. A bare string is sugar for
`{ "name": "<value>" }`; the object form reserves an optional `args`
bag for future per-effect parameters (section 10.4 wire-forward-compat).
v1 effects are parameter-less — `args` MUST be `None` until a
specific effect's registry entry opts into accepting parameters.

EffectDeclaration.description:
One exclusivity-shape effect declaration. A plugin may declare
multiple entries when its experience surfaces have independent
lifecycles; each entry is one consent unit at install time.

asserts.description:
Registered effect names this plugin will assert. Each entry is
either a bare string (sugar for `{ "name": "..." }`) or an object
with `name` and an optional `args` bag reserved for future
per-effect parameters (section 10.4 wire-forward-compat).

conflict_resolution.description:
How concurrent assertions of these effects are arbitrated. v1
ships only `exclusive_last_writer`; the per-effect registry
gates which modes are allowed for each effect name.

user_visible_description.description:
One- or two-sentence description shown in the install-time
consent prompt under `user_visible_name`. Same v1 localization
posture as `user_visible_name`.

user_visible_name.description:
Plugin-facing display name shown in the install-time consent
prompt (e.g. "Focus Mode", "Meeting Mode"). v1: plain string,
English-only. The wire format reserves the structure to evolve
into a locale map without breaking existing manifests.

EventSchemaDecl.description: Schema declaration for an emitted event type.

access.description: Access level: who can subscribe to this event. Default: `open`.

FeedsMatching.description:
Opt-in modes for matching-engine consumption. Closed enum at the platform
level; growth requires a deliberate platform release. See proposal section 3.4.

oneOf.description: Most data — not consumed by the matching engine.
Vocabulary for `<name>` captures during pattern matching.

Vocabulary keyed on the record id, with the whole payload as the
value. Used by auto-registered dynamic collections (collections
whose names are runtime-determined, registered on first
`collection.put`). The record id is what `<name:CollectionName>`
captures match against; the payload travels through the matcher
as the captured value. See notes/DESIGN_BROWSER_HINT_SILENT_EVICTION.md.
Patterns to match against (today's `kind: "commands"`).

Active-context filter. `name_field: None` → gate string is the
collection name (presence-only). `scoped: true` → commands gating
on this collection get priority matching.

Word-level substitutions applied before pattern matching.
Scoped to introducer's commands only (proposal section 9.3).

Names from data records resolvable in `<x>` captures within the
introducer's own action namespace. Cross-plugin requires explicit
grant (proposal section 9.4).

FieldDisplay.description:
Where a field appears in generic UI rendering. Each field on a
collection declares at most one role; surfaces (discovery HUD,
settings UI, etc.) interpret roles on their own terms.

See `notes/DESIGN_COLLECTION_FIELD_ROLES.md` for the full vocabulary
rationale. Roles `primary`, `secondary`, `description`, `payload` are
consumed by `services::matching_service::expand_collections_to_items`
for discovery items; `summary` is consumed by the settings UI's
log-kind timeline rows.

oneOf.description:
The field that identifies the entry — typically the spoken
codeword. Discovery HUD renders as item title. When absent,
surfaces fall back to the entry's spoken key.

The supporting detail shown next to the primary. Discovery HUD
renders as item subtitle.

Long-form explanation. Render shape decided when the first
consumer ships (deferred to Phase 3 settings UI).

Carried for action templates only — never displayed. Stripped
from `DiscoverItem` and any other display surface.

Include this field in the one-line summary shown for each row.
Multiple `summary` fields are concatenated in declaration order.
Pre-existing role; kept for the settings UI's log-kind timeline.

FieldType.description:
The declared shape of a single action-type field. See `ActionFieldSchema`.

Wire format uses snake_case strings (`"string"`, `"int"`, `"enum"`, …).
Unknown values fail deserialization — that's deliberate: a typo in
`plugin.json` should surface at load time, not at dispatch. The `Json`
variant is the documented escape hatch for shapes too dynamic to
declare statically.

ForbidsTagPolicy.description:
`policy.forbids_tag` — defensive negation of `requires_tag`.

Write only commits while the named tag is *not* active. Introducer
and platform writes always pass — only cross-plugin writes are gated.
Intended for plugins that want to defer external writes while a
platform-managed mode is in progress (e.g.
`_platform.calibration.active`).

tag.description:
The exact tag name that must be absent from the active gate set
at write time (for cross-plugin writes).

HudWindowDecl.description: A HUD window declaration from a plugin manifest.

follows_focus.description:
Whether this channel follows the active display on focus changes.
Defaults to `true`.

IdStrategy.description: How records are identified within a collection.

oneOf.description: Auto-generate ULIDs on append (default for `log`).
Use the named field as the record identifier.
Use a (key, value) field pair (for `named_entities` lookups).
At most one record exists; writes replace (default for singleton `tag`).
No identifier — collection is conceptually unordered records.

Lifecycle.description:
When records are deleted by the platform's lifecycle sweep.
`retention_days` lives here (the deletion mechanism), not on `Privacy`.

oneOf.description: Records persist until manually deleted. Default.
Records auto-expire `ttl_ms` milliseconds after creation. Re-setting refreshes.

Records cleared when any of the listed events fires on the event bus.
Sub-sprint 6.3 extended this from a single `event: String` to a list
so scoped+transient namespaces can clear on multiple boundaries (e.g.
both `_platform.input.session_ended` and `_platform.discovery.closed`,
which section 7.2's audit established as genuinely distinct triggers).
Authors writing a single-event lifecycle still use the array form
(`"events": ["x"]`) — keeping the wire shape uniform with the JSON
Schema so external validators agree with the runtime parser.

Records older than `retention_days` deleted by the periodic sweep.
`None` keeps entries until manually deleted.

MergeStrategy.description:
How contributions to a shared store are merged when read.

`Keyed` was removed in Phase 3d of the collection-primitive
unification — it had no legitimate multi-contributor users in
practice; the implicit `Preset::Data` default flipped to
`Authoritative` (matches the dominant single-writer pattern).
`Collect` remains for genuine multi-contributor pooling (keybinds,
`Preset::Tag` multi-cardinality tag collections).

oneOf.description: Only the introducer's contribution is returned. Default.
All contributions are concatenated into a single array, ordered by plugin ordering.

MethodDecl.description: A method declaration: `true` (simple opt-in) or an object (method with metadata).

NetworkConfig.description:
Network access level for macOS sandbox enforcement.
Determines which Seatbelt profile rules are generated.

Ordering.description: Total ordering of records returned by `list()` and `subscribe()`.

oneOf.description: Time-ordered (typically ULID-derived). Default for `log`.
Lexicographic by id field. Default for `named_entities`, `data`.
No defined order.

PlatformConstraint.description: Platform constraint for a stage declaration.

PluginImplements.description:
Methods a plugin declares it implements (actuator → plugin RPC).

`settings_tabs` is a named field because it carries structured metadata (label, key, reads)
and is semantically different from method declarations. All other method names are open-ended
— plugins can declare any method name without actuator code changes.

settings_tabs.description: Settings tabs provided by this plugin (structured metadata, not a method declaration).

PluginSettingsTabDecl.description: A settings tab declared in the plugin manifest.

reads.description: Shared store names this tab needs for rendering (e.g. ["commands", "corrections"]).

Preset.description:
Manifest-level shorthand that resolves to a policy bundle at parse time.
See proposal section 3.5.

oneOf.description:
Active-context flag. `cardinality: singleton` (default) → one record,
owner-only writer; `cardinality: multi` → by-field id, collect merge.

Append-only record store. Per-collection disk, time order, recording
toggle, retention sweep.
Vocabulary collection consumed by matching engine via `as_named_entities`.
Command set consumed by matching engine via `as_command_set`.
Inter-plugin shared state with no special matching-engine processing.

Privacy.description:
Privacy posture declarations. Retention enforcement lives on
`Lifecycle::Retention` — this struct carries policy declarations only.

access_level.description: Read access for cross-plugin reads. Default `open`.

acknowledged_persistence.description:
Suppression slot for state rule 10.2.1: when `true`, declares that
the plugin author has intentionally chosen `lifecycle: persistent`
on a collection with sensitive-shaped fields. Silences the warning
while leaving the privacy claim auditable from the manifest.

default_recording.description: Initial value of the recording toggle on first install.

reason_for_default_on.description:
Suppression slot for state rule 10.2.2: when set, declares why
the plugin needs `default_recording: true` (or
`recording: AlwaysOn`) on a `log`-preset collection. Silences the
warning; the reason text surfaces in the Settings UI inspector
alongside the privacy posture.

recording.description:
Whether records may be recorded at all and whether the user can toggle.
Currently used for log-shaped collections.

Provides.description: What a plugin provides to the ecosystem.

collections.description:
Collections this plugin introduces, keyed by collection name.
Each entry declares schema, merge strategy, access, and optional resolution fields.

context_names.description:
Friendly context names this plugin owns. The context engine's
`name_for(tag_set, partial_prefix)` lookup walks every plugin's
`context_names`; the first pattern whose `tags` are a subset of
the live active-tag set and whose `prefix_shape` matches the
captured prefix positionally wins. See
`notes/DESIGN_CALIBRATION_CONTEXTS.md` §8.

effects.description:
Exclusivity-shape platform effects this plugin participates in.
Each entry declares a set of registered effect names the plugin
will assert during its session, plus the user-facing branding for
the install-time consent prompt. Effect names are validated
against the closed `effects::REGISTERED_EFFECTS` registry at
manifest load time. See `notes/DESIGN_CAPABILITY_MECHANISM.md`.

events.description: Event types this plugin emits, keyed by event type name.

stages.description:
Pipeline stage implementations this plugin contributes, keyed by
plugin-local stage name (e.g. `custom-stt`). The fully-qualified
name on the wire is `<plugin_id>.<stage_name>`.

burst.description:
Maximum burst the bucket can absorb before throttling. Must be > 0
and ≥ `writes_per_second` for the bucket's math to make sense.

writes_per_second.description: Steady-state writes per second the bucket admits. Must be > 0.

RateLimitPolicy.description:
`policy.rate_limit` — manifest payload shape.

Token-bucket params ship in `per_caller`; the bucket's keying is always
`(plugin_id, collection_name)` per design section "Concrete v1 policies →
rate_limit". `introducer_exempt: true` (the default) means the
introducer's own writes don't draw from the bucket — intuitive default
since the introducer is the trust anchor for `_platform.*` and for any
collection it owns. User-state-lowered values override the manifest
declaration when present.

introducer_exempt.description:
When true, writes from the collection's introducer bypass the
bucket. Default true. Plugins that need the introducer rate-limited
(e.g. testing the limit applies to themselves) set this to false.

RecordingPolicy.description: Whether recording is user-toggleable or platform-enforced.

oneOf.description: User can toggle recording on/off in the Settings UI.
Always recording, no user toggle.
Recording disabled, plugin cannot append.

RequiresTagPolicy.description:
`policy.requires_tag` — gate-tag declaration.

Specifies a tag name that must be active at write time. Different
from `feeds_matching: AsGates` (which describes how *this* collection
produces gates) — this is the inverse: what gate is required to write
here. Avoid declaring `requires_tag` on a collection whose own writes
set/clear the named tag (self-referential gate).

tag.description:
The exact tag name (e.g. `"_platform.input.active"`) that must be
in the active gate set when a write reaches the chain.

StageDeclaration.description:
One stage implementation declared in a plugin manifest's `provides.stages`.
The HashMap key in `Provides::stages` is the plugin-local stage name (e.g.
`custom-stt`); the fully-qualified name on the wire and in pipeline records
is `<plugin_id>.<stage_name>`.

The plugin must also implement the corresponding RPC handler for the
streaming dispatch path (step 15.4). The actuator cannot statically
verify handler presence — runtime invocation surfaces missing handlers.

binary.description:
Binary name to spawn for this stage. Resolved relative to the plugin
directory. If omitted, defaults to the stage name.

parameters.description:
Parameter schema for this stage. Reuses the existing
`ActionFieldSchema` / `FieldType` machinery — same algebra applied
to a new dimension. Empty if the stage takes no parameters.

platform.description: Platform constraint. Omitted = available on all platforms.

priority.description:
Resolution priority when multiple stages match a type-based reference.
Higher wins. Default 0.

stage_type.description:
Plugin-defined stage type string. The platform uses this for
type-based resolution (`*.stt`) — matching producers to consumers.
Plugins define their own vocabulary (e.g. `stt`, `tts`, `resolver`).

Storage.description: Backend that holds the collection's records.

oneOf.description: Per-collection JSON file in app support dir. Default for `log`, `command_set`.

Stored in shared collection_overrides backend. Default for
`named_entities`, `data`.
In-memory only, lost on restart. Default for `tag`.
In-memory ring buffer with maximum size; oldest evicted on overflow.

Subscription.description: A subscription pattern for consuming events.

UiRender.description: How the collection renders in the Settings UI's Collections tab.

oneOf.description: Editable rows-and-columns table. Default for `named_entities`.
Time-ordered timeline with summary fields. Default for `log`.
One-line summary card. Default for `data`.
Command list with patterns and bindings. Default for `command_set`.

Read-only tag-namespace listing with current active gates.
Default for `tag` — surfaces tag-shape collections in the
Collections tab (state doc Phase D). A plugin shipping
custom UI for a tag namespace overrides this slot.

Hidden from the Collections tab. An explicit "don't surface"
escape hatch — no preset defaults to this.

Writers.description: Who is allowed to write records to this collection.

oneOf.description: Only the introducer plugin. Default for `tag`, `log`.

Any plugin that declares the collection in `consumes` (and contributes
via `collection_data`). Default for `named_entities`, `command_set`,
`data`.
Only the platform itself; plugins read but cannot write.

description: Declares what a plugin does. BranchKit reads plugin.json at startup to configure routing, dependency graphs, store ownership, and UI.

action_prefix.description: Action routing prefix claimed by this plugin (e.g. "voice", "wm").

action_prefix_access.description:
Access level for this plugin's action prefix — who can dispatch to it.
Default: `open` (declaring an action prefix is inherently a public contract).

action_types.description:
Action type schemas declared by this plugin (e.g. "click" → {fields: [...]}).
Keys are the action name without prefix (e.g. "click" not "browser.click").
The full action type is formed by joining action_prefix + "." + key.

collection_data.description:
Collection data sources: maps collection name → data source (file path or inline JSON).
Having an entry here implicitly means the plugin contributes to that collection,
creating a dependency on the collection's introducer in the dep graph.

consumes.description: What this plugin consumes from the ecosystem.

default_footer.description: Default HUD footer text when this plugin's commands are shown.

dispatch_prefixes.description: When `dispatch_via` is `direct`, the action prefixes this plugin targets.

dispatch_via.description:
How this plugin determines dispatch targets (commands, action_types, or direct).
Requires `dispatch` privilege.

grammar_seeds.description:
Declared vocabulary sources for collections this plugin's commands
capture from. Maps a target collection name — or a dynamic-collection
family, written with a trailing `*` (e.g. `"grid_cells_*"`) — to a
source collection whose spoken keys are unioned into the target's
grammar high-water mark at load time and on every rebuild of the
source. Pre-fills the engine grammar so it is converged at launch;
the HWM keeps learning on top (the seed is a floor, not a ceiling).
Cross-plugin sources are allowed. See DESIGN_GRAMMAR_ALPHABET_SEED.md.

hud_windows.description:
HUD windows this plugin wants to display. Each entry auto-creates a
broadcast channel on plugin load. See `hud_channels::HudWindowDecl`.

min_api_version.description:
Minimum API version this plugin requires (e.g. "0.1.0").
Required — the actuator rejects the plugin at load time if omitted or if API_VERSION < min_api_version.

network.description:
Network access level for sandbox enforcement.
Omitted = no network (tightest sandbox). See DESIGN_PLUGIN_SANDBOX.md.

provides.description: What this plugin provides to the ecosystem.

publisher.description:
Publisher identity anchored at a git provider, `<provider>:<identity>`
(or `<provider>=<host>:<identity>` for self-hosted). This is the trust
anchor — unlike `author` (free-text, anyone can set), a `publisher` is
what the signing chain's author signature and the verified-publisher
badge resolve against. Optional; absent = no provenance claim (community
/ unsigned posture). Parse with `parsed_publisher()`. See
notes/DESIGN_PLUGIN_SIGNING_CHAIN.md + DESIGN_PLUGIN_PUBLISHER_VERIFICATION.md.

run.description: Shell command to run the plugin process. When set, the actuator spawns + monitors this process.

title: BranchKit Plugin Manifest
